w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring 16 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs and 96 vCPUs for massive parallel processing and high-performance computing workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g: Accelerator-optimized server with 16 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 1360GB RAM for intensive workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 1360GB of RAM, designed for massive parallel processing and AI training tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server instance built on an x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs derived from 48 physical Intel Xeon cores operating at 2.2 GHz, supported by 1360 GB of system memory. The configuration includes 16 NVIDIA Tesla A100 Ampere GPUs providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth, though single-core performance metrics are lower. The system supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, and BMI2, alongside security features like IBRS and SSBD. This server is engineered for compute-intensive tasks requiring massive parallel acceleration, such as large-scale machine learning model training, complex scientific simulations, and high-performance data analytics workloads where GPU throughput is the primary requirement.
